round_1m_discretionary_arms_length.pred: amount >= 100000 AND is_round_1m AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD','TRAVEL - IN-STATE','TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
round_1m_discretionary_arms_length.guard: (1) Category whitelist EXCLUDES entitlement/scheduled families (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS, PERSONAL SERVICES) whose round amounts are benign-by-construction. (2) Arms-length filter: agency<>payee AND word-BOUNDARY (regexp_matches with \b, never blind substring — the ZTE/Aztec lesson) name-containment in either direction, to suppress same-entity book transfers that evade exact-equality (e.g. UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA -> 'THE UNIVERSITY OF ARIZONA'/'...(FINANCIAL SERVICES OFFICE)', PSPRS self-pay). Name overlap is used only to SUPPRESS (never to flag), so substring risk is one-directional/conservative. (3) Excludes INTERNAL/JOURNAL VOUCHER/COST ALLOCATION rails (non-arms-length fund movements). (4) Junk/placeholder payees ('N/A' etc.) excluded. (5) amount>=100000 floor. No dependence on thin-year-NULL fields, so it degrades gracefully and never penalizes a row for a system-wide absent field. These guardrails removed ~48% of raw round_1m discretionary hits (213 -> 110) that were internal/self transfers.

round_100k_sub1m_discretionary_arms_leng.pred: amount >= 100000 AND is_round_100k AND NOT is_round_1m AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD','TRAVEL - IN-STATE','TRAVEL - OUT-OF-STATE') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
round_100k_sub1m_discretionary_arms_leng.guard: Identical guardrail stack to the $1M marker: discretionary-only category whitelist (excludes entitlement/scheduled families that are legitimately round), word-boundary same-entity suppression (regexp \b, never blind substring), INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rail exclusion, junk-payee exclusion, amount>=100000 floor, no thin-year-NULL field dependence. NOT is_round_1m enforces clean tiering vs the severity-5 marker (no double-flagging the same row).

whole_dollar_noncents_discretionary.pred: amount >= 100000 AND is_whole_dollar AND NOT is_round_10k AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES','FOOD') AND (payee IS NULL OR (agency <> payee AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(payee), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(agency)) || '\b') AND NOT regexp_matches(upper(agency), '\b' || regexp_escape(upper(payee)) || '\b'))) AND upper(coalesce(payee,'X')) NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','NONE','VARIOUS') AND (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method NOT IN ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'))
whole_dollar_noncents_discretionary.guard: (1) Category whitelist restricted to the SIX categories empirically shown to carry cents by default — DEBT SERVICE (44% cents) and TRANSFERS OUT (47% cents) and PERSONAL SERVICES/payroll-driven categories are excluded precisely because whole-dollar amounts there are normal, not anomalous. (2) NOT is_round_10k removes the obviously/legitimately-round purchase-order amounts (those are the domain of the round-100k/1m markers) so this tier isolates the 'oddly exact whole number' band. (3) Same word-boundary arms-length same-entity suppression (this guardrail alone removed 719 intra-University-of-Arizona self-transfers + 457 'N/A' placeholder payees that otherwise dominated the raw output). (4) INTERNAL/JV/COST-ALLOCATION rail exclusion. (5) amount>=100000 floor; no thin-year-NULL dependence. Confirmed not concentrated in a single benign payer after guardrails; FY distribution is flat ~720-1090/yr across 2016-2025.

dup_diffday_discretionary.pred: amount >= 100000 AND in_dup_diffday AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE')
dup_diffday_discretionary.guard: Excludes entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = Medicaid capitation; TRANSFERS OUT; DEBT SERVICE) where same-reference repeats are legitimate. No name/substring matching is used (signature is structural: id+amount+reference), so the Aztec/ZTE false-positive class cannot occur. Lead only - identical amounts paid weeks apart may be contractually-scheduled installments under one PO; confirm against the contract/invoice before drawing any conclusion. Names no wrongdoer.

dup_sameday_discretionary_external.pred: amount >= 100000 AND in_dup_signature AND NOT in_dup_diffday AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND payee <> agency
dup_sameday_discretionary_external.guard: payee <> agency suppresses the pension-paydown / intra-fund self-payment batches the brief calls out as benign (PSPRS, fund-to-fund). Entitlement categories excluded. Structural signature match only - no name/substring logic, so no Aztec/ZTE-class risk. Same-day exact repeats are frequently legitimate invoice splits; this is an item to confirm against AP detail, never an accusation.

dup_diffday_all_incl_entitlement.guard: Severity deliberately low because the population is dominated by entitlement spend where same-reference batches are legitimate (Medicaid capitation, provider recoupments, DES transfers). Use only as a coverage net, not a standalone red flag; route entitlement hits to a lighter reconciliation queue. Structural match only - no name matching. Names no wrongdoer.

sole_source_discretionary_concentration.pred: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.20 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','EQUIPMENT','SUPPLIES AND MATERIALS','TRAVEL') AND NOT (payee = agency OR payee ILIKE 'STATE OF ARIZONA%' OR (payee ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%') OR (payee ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%' AND agency ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%')) AND NOT (regexp_matches(payee, '(^| )(U S BANK|BANK OF AMERICA|WELLS FARGO|JPMORGAN|BNY MELLON|NORTHERN TRUST)( |$|,)') OR payee ILIKE '%RETIREMENT SYSTEM%')
sole_source_discretionary_concentration.guard: (1) Entitlement/scheduled categories excluded by construction (only discretionary category1 values included) per the auditor risk-weighting brief. (2) Self-payments / intra-governmental transfers suppressed: payee=agency, 'STATE OF ARIZONA*' (school-finance pass-throughs via Dept of Education), university-to-self, community-college-to-self -- these dominate the high-share band and are benign-by-construction. (3) Banks and pension systems suppressed via WORD-BOUNDARY regexp (anchored on '^| ' and a trailing space/comma/end), explicitly so 'BANNER HEALTH' does NOT match 'BANK' -- this is the Aztec/ZTE substring-trap class of error and was unit-tested (BANK OF AMERICA matches, BANNER HEALTH does not). (4) vid IS NOT NULL guarantees the marker cannot fire on FY2019/2020 or on any row whose vendor identity is unresolved, so it never penalizes a system-wide-absent field. (5) amount>=100000 keeps headline stats on the institutional-materiality population. Residual benign risk: a legitimate statewide-sole-provider in a tiny single-purpose commission (e.g. PETSMART CHARITIES at the Spay/Neuter Committee) can appear -- treat as dependency, confirm via contract, do not escalate.

vendor_agency_dependency_exposure.pred: amount >= 100000 AND vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.33 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE') AND NOT (payee = agency OR payee ILIKE 'STATE OF ARIZONA%' OR (payee ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%' AND agency ILIKE '%UNIVERSITY%') OR (payee ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%' AND agency ILIKE '%COMMUNITY COLLEGE%'))
vendor_agency_dependency_exposure.guard: (1) Entitlement categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) excluded -- these go high for scheduled/benefit reasons (e.g. STATE OF ARIZONA school-finance disbursements through Dept of Education) and carry low per-txn suspicion. (2) Self-payment / intra-governmental suppression identical to marker 1. (3) Banks/pension/utilities INTENTIONALLY retained (not suppressed) and labeled dependency, with the lower severity weight encoding 'exposure not irregularity'. (4) vid IS NOT NULL prevents firing on thin years or unresolved vendors. (5) Because vendor_share is whole-agency, this is an exposure lens, not a procurement-competition lens -- do not read a high share alone as evidence of anything improper.

new_vendor_dominant_agency_share.pred: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_first_appearance AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND vendor_year_total >= 1000000 AND vendor_share_of_agency_year >= 0.10 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
new_vendor_dominant_agency_share.guard: (1) vid IS NOT NULL required so the marker NEVER fires in thin years FY2019/2020 where vendor identity is system-wide NULL. (2) fiscal_year>2016 excludes the left-censoring artifact: FY2016 is the first data year so every vendor reads as first-appearance (e.g. ASU paying itself $90M lump-sum mislabeled 'new'). (3) Entitlement/scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = Medicaid/benefits, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION) are excluded - a new Medicaid MCO like Bridgeway Health Solutions at $59M is a scheduled capitation, not a procurement red flag. (4) LUMP SUM appropriations excluded - these are intra-government university/agency operating transfers, not arm's-length vendor onboarding. (5) CAVEAT, confirm before escalating: FY2021 is inflated by the post-gap re-appearance effect (vendors untracked during the FY2019/2020 NULL-vid gap re-surface as 'first appearance'); FY2021 leads need an independent corroborant (prior-period spend, contract record) before being treated as truly new. (6) Benign-match suppression to verify on review: legitimate competitively-awarded new contracts, renamed/merged entities reusing a new vid, one-off capital grants to municipalities, and federal pass-through grantees. This is a LEAD WARRANTING REVIEW, not a finding; it names no wrongdoer. Any future name-based refinement must use word-boundary matching, never substring (cf. the Aztec/ZTE false-positive class).

new_vendor_large_first_payment_discretio.pred: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_first_appearance AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
new_vendor_large_first_payment_discretio.guard: Same structural guardrails as the dominant-share marker: vid IS NOT NULL (never fires thin years), fiscal_year>2016 (drops FY2016 left-censoring), entitlement categories excluded (AID/TRANSFERS OUT/DEBT SERVICE), LUMP SUM excluded. HEAVY CAVEAT: 73% of this marker's rows fall in FY2021, driven by the post-NULL-vid-gap re-appearance artifact rather than true newness - treat FY2021 hits as low-confidence pending an independent prior-spend check; the ~1,400 non-FY2021 rows are the trustworthy core. Expected benign matches to confirm-not-accuse: legitimately competed new awards, renamed/merged entities, one-off municipal capital grants, federal pass-through grantees. Lead-not-finding framing; no name substring matching (word-boundary only).

concentrated_short_tenure_vendor.pred: vid IS NOT NULL AND vendor_years_active <= 2 AND vendor_year_total >= 2000000 AND fiscal_year > 2016 AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND NOT (upper(appropriation_1_name) LIKE '%LUMP SUM%')
concentrated_short_tenure_vendor.guard: vid IS NOT NULL (no thin-year firing), fiscal_year>2016 (no left-censoring), LUMP SUM excluded, entitlement categories already excluded by the narrow category list. Note: legitimate short-duration construction/engineering JVs are common in ADOT capital programs and are the dominant benign class here - the marker flags them as items to confirm (is this a real competitively-awarded JV with bonded principals?), never as findings. vendor_years_active is computed only over years where the vendor is observable; the FY2019/2020 gap can understate tenure, so a vendor reading '2 years active' may have been active longer - corroborate tenure before treating short tenure as suspicious. Lead-not-finding; word-boundary not substring for any name logic.

p13_adjustment_period_highvalue.guard: Item to confirm, not a finding: the dominant content is legitimate scheduled true-ups -- DEBT SERVICE ($571M, median $66.7M, clearly scheduled), AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS ($1.64B Medicaid/benefit accruals) and TRANSFERS OUT ($562M). These are expected-large entitlement/scheduled categories and should be DOWN-weighted (treat as informational, not severe) when reviewing. Never fires in FY19/20 because fp_canon IS NULL there. No name matching used, so no ZTE/Aztec-class substring risk. Do not penalize a row merely for NULL payee here -- NULL payee is the structural norm for period-13 book entries.

p13_discretionary_closing_entry.pred: amount >= 100000 AND fp_canon = 13 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
p13_discretionary_closing_entry.guard: Item to confirm, not an accusation: legitimate year-end accruals for services delivered-not-yet-invoiced and IT/automation true-ups appear here (e.g. court 'AUTOMATION', 'IBM LEGACY PROJECT'). Reviewer should confirm an accrual basis and that goods/services were received by June 30. Entitlement categories are excluded by construction so the marker never penalizes scheduled benefit spending. Never fires FY19/20. No substring name matching. NULL/'N/A' payee is structurally normal for these book entries and must not itself raise severity.

june_useitorloseit_round_discretionary.pred: amount >= 100000 AND fp_canon = 12 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES') AND (is_round_100k OR is_round_1m)
june_useitorloseit_round_discretionary.guard: Item to confirm, not a finding: round-dollar June payments are frequently legitimate (grant disbursements, contract milestone draws, intergovernmental transfers). Notable benign-by-pattern subset: PSPRS booking ten identical round $99M 'Unfunded Liability Reduction' self-payments (agency=payee) in June FY2021 -- scheduled pension contributions, low suspicion despite firing. Excludes entitlement/scheduled categories (AID, DEBT SERVICE, TRANSFERS OUT). To prioritize the cleanest external leads, reviewers may add 'AND payee <> agency AND vid IS NOT NULL' (filters intra-government and thin-year rows). Never fires FY19/20. No substring name matching -- only exact category-set and numeric round flags, so no ZTE/Aztec-class false positives.

manual_rail_discretionary_procurement.pred: payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
manual_rail_discretionary_procurement.guard: Entitlement/scheduled categories are EXCLUDED by construction: the predicate whitelists only the four discretionary procurement category1 values, so it never fires on TRANSFERS OUT (10,013 INTERNAL rows / $20.2B), AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, DEBT SERVICE, or COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS - exactly the inter-fund JV/INTERNAL traffic the family caveat warns about. No name/substring matching is involved (rail + category enums only), so the ZTE/Aztec false-positive class cannot occur. Produces leads to confirm, not findings. Note OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES still contains some scheduled COP debt-service paydowns labeled under that category (e.g. DOA 'COP DEBT SERVICE' INTERNAL entries); reviewers should de-prioritize rows whose appropriation_1_name contains a debt-service token - this is a refinement, not a fraud determination.

manual_rail_nonentitlement_broad.pred: payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 NOT IN ('TRANSFERS OUT','AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','DEBT SERVICE','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS')
manual_rail_nonentitlement_broad.guard: Excludes the four scheduled/entitlement categories (TRANSFERS OUT, AID, DEBT SERVICE, COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS) where JV/INTERNAL is the normal mechanism - this is the explicit inter-fund-transfer guardrail from the family brief. Pure enum logic, no name matching, so no substring false-positive risk. Lower severity (3) than the discretionary marker because PERSONAL SERVICES / EMPLOYEE RELATED / TRAVEL manual entries are more often legitimate payroll-adjacent reclassifications; treat as a corroborating signal, not standalone. Leads to confirm only.

nonstandard_rail_null_or_warrant_discret.pred: (payment_method IS NULL OR payment_method = 'WARRANT') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
nonstandard_rail_null_or_warrant_discret.guard: Restricted to the discretionary procurement categories so the very large NULL-rail entitlement traffic (AID 28,588 rows / $59B, TRANSFERS 5,876 rows / $21.7B) is excluded - those would otherwise swamp the marker. Because NULL is common and partly benign, this marker is intended only as a low-weight corroborator that should be combined with another red flag (e.g. vendor_first_appearance, round_100k, robust_z>=10) before escalation; do NOT treat a NULL rail alone as suspicious. No name matching, so no substring false-positive risk. Leads to confirm only.

peer_outlier_discretionary_zextreme.pred: amount >= 100000 AND robust_z_in_agencycat >= 20 AND ac_n >= 8 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE','COST ALLOCATION AND INDIRECT COSTS','PERSONAL SERVICES','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES')
peer_outlier_discretionary_zextreme.guard: (1) EXCLUDES entitlement / scheduled categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS = AHCCCS/Medicaid capitation, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) plus COST ALLOCATION, PERSONAL SERVICES and EMPLOYEE RELATED - these are the documented mega-program benign outliers (AHCCCS Prop 204 capitation hit z up to 3,864 for benign reasons; 33,108 of 44,158 z>=10 rows are entitlement). (2) Requires ac_n >= 8 so z is computed against a stable peer group, not 2-3 rows (only 118 rows in pop have ac_n<8, so this barely costs coverage but prevents unstable-denominator artifacts). (3) Pure-amount signal, so no name-matching false-positive class here; the Aztec/ZTE substring lesson does not apply but is respected by avoiding any LIKE on payee. (4) This is a LEAD, not a finding: a high z legitimately occurs for one-off emergency procurement (e.g. AshBritt border-fence) - the marker says 'confirm the contracting basis,' it does not assert impropriety.

generic_placeholder_payee_on_discretiona.pred: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND (payee IN ('N/A','MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR','MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER') OR payee IS NULL OR regexp_matches(upper(payee), '(^| )(PCARD|FUEL) VENDOR( |$)'))
generic_placeholder_payee_on_discretiona.guard: WORD-BOUNDARY / EXACT-MATCH ONLY to avoid the ZTE/Aztec false-positive class: payee tokens are matched by exact equality ('N/A' etc.) or regex with explicit word boundaries '(^| )(PCARD|FUEL) VENDOR( |$)' -- NEVER a substring LIKE '%TEMP%' (which spuriously hit CITY OF TEMPE, TEMPLE EMANUEL, CONTEMPORARY SERVICES). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES the literal string payee 'NULL' and 'NAME REDACTED': those are concentrated in AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS / TRANSFERS OUT / PERSONAL SERVICES (privacy-redacted benefit recipients and payroll) and are benign-by-construction, not procurement gaps. Restricting category1 to discretionary procurement excludes entitlement/scheduled spend where a missing individual name is expected. No vid/appr_type/fp dependence, so it degrades gracefully and never penalizes a thin-year field.

triple_gap_nullvid_manualrail_noPayee_di.pred: amount >= 100000 AND fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) AND vid IS NULL AND payment_method IN ('JOURNAL VOUCHER','INTERNAL') AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT')
triple_gap_nullvid_manualrail_noPayee_di.guard: vid IS NULL is REQUIRED and fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) is hard-coded, so the marker can NEVER fire merely because vid is system-wide absent in a thin year (the critical degrade-gracefully requirement). Manual-rail restricted to JOURNAL VOUCHER/INTERNAL (not ACH/WARRANT, which are normal vendor payments). Discretionary category filter excludes entitlement/scheduled spend. Because it is a subset of the generic_placeholder_payee marker, in a combined score it should be treated as an ESCALATOR of those rows rather than additive double-counting -- document the overlap so the two are not summed independently.

large_procurement_no_contract_reference.pred: amount >= 1000000 AND fiscal_year NOT IN (2019,2020) AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','CAPITAL OUTLAY') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND payee IS NOT NULL AND payee NOT IN ('N/A','NULL','MISCELLANEOUS VENDOR','MISCELLANEOUS CUSTOMER') AND upper(trim(payee)) <> upper(trim(agency))
large_procurement_no_contract_reference.guard: MODERATE severity (2), NOT severe, because contract_number is only ~2-6% populated mart-wide -- absence may reflect a data-feed limitation rather than a truly missing contract, so this is explicitly an 'item to confirm,' never an assertion that no contract exists. vid IS NOT NULL is REQUIRED, which both ensures a real named vendor and makes the marker dark in thin years (never fires on a system-wide-absent field). Self-payments are excluded via upper(trim(payee)) <> upper(trim(agency)) -- this removed a benign-by-construction artifact where PSPRS paid ITSELF $99M for 'unfunded liability reduction' (an internal funding transfer, not a procurement, so no contract is expected). Generic payee tokens excluded so it does not double-count marker A. Category restricted to true procurement (PROFESSIONAL/CAPITAL OUTLAY); OTHER OPERATING was dropped because it mixed in non-procurement internal transfers.

disc_proc_offcontract_base.pred: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND contract_number IS NULL AND vid IS NOT NULL AND coalesce(category2,'') <> 'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' AND payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency
disc_proc_offcontract_base.guard: (1) vid IS NOT NULL requires a REAL vendor id, which both proves the payee is an external vendor (not a sentinel/intra-state shell) AND auto-suppresses the marker in thin years FY2019/FY2020 where vid is NULL system-wide -- so we never penalize a transaction for a field that is absent by year. (2) coalesce(category2,'')<>'INTRASTATE DISTRIBUTIONS' removes benign intra-governmental distributions miscoded under OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES (e.g. PSPRS pension unfunded-liability transfers). (3) payee IS DISTINCT FROM agency removes self-payee internal transfers (NULL-safe). (4) Category whitelist (not a substring match) avoids the ZTE/Aztec class of error -- we match exact category1 strings, never substrings of names. (5) contract_number is only ~8-30% populated even in good years, so absence is the screening signal, NOT proof of off-contract spend; an absent linkage in this field can mean the contract exists but was not keyed -- hence moderate severity and 'item to confirm', not accusation. Known residual: ADOT CAPITAL OUTLAY construction frequently runs on engineering/construction contracts whose number is simply not linked in this field; reviewers should de-prioritize ADOT FEDERAL AID / M-RARF construction draws.

person_name_payee_in_discretionary.pred: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES') AND payee ~ '^[A-Z][A-Z''-]+ [A-Z][A-Z''-]+$' AND NOT regexp_matches(payee, '(INC|LLC|LLP|CORP|COMPANY|LTD|PLLC|ASSOC|GROUP|TRUST|BANK|COUNTY|UNIV|DEPT|SERVICE|SYSTEM|SOLUTION|TECH|CONSULT|ENTERPRISE|HOLDING|PARTNER|FUND|FOUNDATION|HOSPITAL|CLINIC|CENTER|CENTRE|INSTITUTE|SCHOOL|DISTRICT|HEALTH|MEDICAL|INSURANCE|CONSTRUCT|ELECTRIC|SUPPLY|EQUIP|PRODUCT|INDUSTR|MGMT|MANAGEMENT|CAPITAL|FINANCIAL|REALTY|PROPERT|MOTOR|AUTO|ENERGY|POWER|WATER|COMMUNICAT|MEDIA|PRESS|ENGINEER|ARCHITECT|PHARM|NETWORK|SOFTWARE|DESIGN|CONTRACT|BUILDER|FARM|RANCH|MARKET|STORE|FOOD|INTERNATIONAL|NATIONAL|GLOBAL|AMERICA|DIGITAL|CREATIVE|DEVELOP|LEARNING|DISTRIBUT|NEXTEL|INSIGHT|VENDOR|CUSTOMER|MISC|VARIOUS|REDACTED|TRANSPORT|MINISTR|CASINO|RISK|LENS|PIPELINE|STORAGE|TRAURIG|ROCK|CHEVROLET|KENWORTH)')
person_name_payee_in_discretionary.guard: (1) WORD-BOUNDARY/regexp_matches business-keyword exclusion (NOT substring) directly answers the ZTE/Aztec failure mode: company names containing INC/LLC/CORP/COUNTY/HEALTH/etc. are excluded so 'PITNEY BOWES'/'RR DONNELLEY'/'GRANITE CONSTRUCTION' do not fire. (2) Scoped to DISCRETIONARY categories only; entitlement/benefit categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) and PERSONAL SERVICES payroll are excluded, so privacy-redacted benefit recipients are never flagged. (3) Excludes 'NAME REDACTED'/'N/A' sentinels via the keyword list. (4) SEVERITY HELD AT 3 (not 5) BY DESIGN: legitimate sole proprietors, individual contractors, expert witnesses, and litigation-settlement payees with common surnames exist; this is a lead-to-confirm, never an accusation. (5) CONFIRMATION STEP (recommended, requires a feature not in mart): validate split_part(payee,' ',1) against a US Census/SSA given-name gazetteer (a needs_firstname_gazetteer flag) to raise precision and drop two-token brand names like 'CUSTOM STORAGE'/'ORIGAMI RISK' that survive the keyword filter. With a gazetteer the fire set tightens further (~23 rows on a tiny demo list), so the structural predicate here is the recall-side net and the gazetteer is the precision-side confirm. (6) Independent of vid/appr_type, so it never penalizes a thin-year (FY19/20) row for a system-wide-absent field.

placeholder_masked_payee_external_rail_d.pred: amount >= 100000 AND category1 IN ('PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES','OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES','CAPITAL OUTLAY','CAPITAL EQUIPMENT','NON-CAPITAL EQUIPMENT') AND payment_method IN ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') AND (payee IS NULL OR upper(payee) IN ('N/A','NA','NONE','NULL','UNKNOWN','TBD','VOID') OR regexp_matches(payee, '(^|\s)(MISC|MISCELLANEOUS|VARIOUS|VENDOR|CUSTOMER|PAYEE|PLACEHOLDER|TEMP|TEMPORARY|TEST|DUMMY|DEFAULT|SUNDRY|PCARD|FUEL VENDOR)(\s|$)'))
placeholder_masked_payee_external_rail_d.guard: (1) CRITICAL RAIL GUARDRAIL: restrict to external rails ('ACH','WARRANT','PAYMENT') and EXCLUDE internal-accounting rails ('INTERNAL','JOURNAL VOUCHER','COST ALLOCATION'). Without this the marker balloons to ~14,900 rows / $8.9B because 'N/A' is the normal, benign placeholder on internal cost-allocation and inter-fund transfer entries (no external payee exists by design). The rail filter is what converts a benign-by-construction artifact into a real lead. (2) Scoped to discretionary procurement categories only; entitlement/transfer/debt-service categories where placeholders are routine are excluded. (3) WORD-BOUNDARY regex ((^|\s)...(\s|$)) so 'VENDOR'/'CUSTOMER' match only as standalone tokens and never as a substring inside a legitimate firm name (e.g. avoids flagging a real company whose name contains 'VENDOR'). (4) Independent of vid/appr_type, so no thin-year penalty. (5) Severity 3, not 5: many of these are legitimate pooled/p-card or escrow-style aggregations and broker-commission clearing accounts — each is an item to confirm (who was actually paid?), not a finding.

agency_benford_digit_nonconformity.pred: agency_benford_flag = TRUE AND amount >= 100000 AND category1 NOT IN ('AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS','TRANSFERS OUT','DEBT SERVICE')
agency_benford_digit_nonconformity.guard: (1) ENTITLEMENT EXCLUSION: even within a flagged agency, scheduled/entitlement categories (AID TO ORGANIZATIONS AND INDIVIDUALS, TRANSFERS OUT, DEBT SERVICE) are excluded — large scheduled amounts are expected and Benford conformity is not a fraud signal there; this drops exposure from $5.24B to $1.97B by removing $3.0B of TRANSFERS OUT. (2) CORROBORATION: requires BOTH first- AND second-digit MAD above peer p75, so a single-test outlier cannot trigger the flag (prevents the NAU-type false positive). (3) MINIMUM SAMPLE: n>=500 high-value txns per agency, so digit frequencies are statistically meaningful and small agencies cannot be flagged on noise. (4) NO NAME MATCHING: this marker keys on agency name as an exact categorical grouping, not substring — so it is immune to the 'Aztec'->'ZTE' substring class of error. (5) THIN-YEAR SAFE: uses no field that is NULL in FY2019/2020. (6) LOW WEIGHT: severity 2, never standalone — it elevates an agency's transactions modestly and should only escalate when combined with a transaction-level signal (round-amount, vendor-concentration, duplicate). (7) PAYROLL CAVEAT (note, not hard-coded): PERSONAL SERVICES is the largest discretionary category in the flagged set ($882M); payroll amounts follow salary schedules, so reviewers should down-weight digit signals on PERSONAL SERVICES / EMPLOYEE RELATED EXPENDITURES and focus on PROFESSIONAL AND OUTSIDE SERVICES, OTHER OPERATING EXPENDITURES, CAPITAL OUTLAY/EQUIPMENT (procurement-only subset = 2,763 txns / $846.6M).
